Name:  ___                   Unit No:   ___
 
Admission Date:  ___              Discharge Date:   ___
 
Date of Birth:  ___             Sex:   F
 
Service: SURGERY
 
Allergies: 
clarithromycin / Mucinex
 
Attending: ___.
 
Chief Complaint:
metastatic melanoma
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
excision of metastatic melanoma left shoulder, left axillary 
lymph node dissection, excision of basal cell carcinoma of 
abdomen. 

 
History of Present Illness:
Pt is a pleasant ___ F with hx of severe oxygen-dependent COPD, 
CAD s/p CABG, DM, and morbid obesity, with a recent diagnosis of 
melanoma of her left anterior shoulder who is admitted for 
pre-op optimization followed by wide excision left shoulder 
melanoma, excision left axillary lymph node, and basal
cell carcinoma of her abdomen. On ___ a shave biopsy of 
her left shoulder showed invasive nodular melanoma with minimal 
depth of 3.15 mm and extending to ___ level IV. She has severe 
COPD and the day following her diagnosis she was admitted to OSH 
for bibasilar PNA, found to have bulky left axillary mass with 
needle biopsy consistent with melanoma. On her admission today 
she is feeling well, and her daughter reports she is now "the 
best she has been in over ___ years" in terms of her 
functionalstatus and requiring only 2L NC at the moment. 

 
Past Medical History:
Past Medical / Surgical History: DM II, severe COPD, OA, CAD s/p
CABG, T3a melonoma on L arm with nodal involvement, HL,
Depression, Hx SVT, details unclear, cataract surgery, s/p
splenectomy after MVA, PAD, Obesity, Colonic adenoma,

 
Social History:
___
Family History:
___ family history is not significant for melanoma. Her 
sister had cancer of unknown type.  Other family members have 
coronary artery disease and diabetes.

 
Physical Exam:
On admission: General: NAD, lying down comfortably in bed. 
Conversational and
pleasant. 
Vital signs stable
Cardiac: RRR (74bpm), no murmurs. Healed prior sternotomy. 
Pulm: Breathing normally and comfortably on 2L NC, resp rate 14.
No wheezes. 
Abd: soft, obese, nontender, nondistended, healed prior midline
incision.  Approx 1cm ulcerative lesion(basal cell carcinoma) on
upper abdomen in midline. 
Ext: Prior cellulitis improved, some erythema over anterior 
right
shin. WWP. No calf tenderness. 2+ DP. 
Left shoulder: approx 0.5 cm dark irregular brown-colored
melanoma lesion over anterior aspect of shoulder with some
surrounding erythema. 
Left axilla: palpable bulky mass which feels circular/ovoid in
nature, approx 10cm in size although difficult to assess, mildly
tender to touch, mobile. 

On discharge: 
General: NAD, lying down comfortably in bed. Conversational and
pleasant. 
Vital signs stable (O2 sat 97% on 3L NC)
Cardiac: RRR, no murmurs. Healed prior sternotomy. 
Pulm: Breathing normally and comfortably on 2L NC, resp rate 14.
No wheezes. 
Abd: soft, obese, nontender, nondistended. 
Ext: Prior cellulitis improved, some erythema over anterior 
right
shin. WWP. No calf tenderness. 2+ DP. 
Incisions: dressings on left shoulder as well as upper abdomen 
c/d/i, no erythema/drainage or signs of infection. JP drain in 
place with surrounding clean dressing. 190cc serosanguinous 
fluid over past 24 hours. 
 
Pertinent Results:
___ 07:10AM BLOOD WBC-12.7* RBC-4.15* Hgb-12.2 Hct-39.3 
MCV-95 MCH-29.4 MCHC-31.0 RDW-14.7 Plt ___
___ 07:00AM BLOOD WBC-10.9 RBC-4.27 Hgb-12.7 Hct-39.6 
MCV-93 MCH-29.8 MCHC-32.1 RDW-14.6 Plt ___
___ 06:50PM BLOOD WBC-12.8* RBC-4.42 Hgb-12.8 Hct-40.9 
MCV-93 MCH-28.9 MCHC-31.2 RDW-14.7 Plt ___
___ 07:10AM BLOOD Glucose-139* UreaN-15 Creat-1.3* Na-139 
K-4.0 Cl-97 HCO___-33* AnGap-13
___ 07:10AM BLOOD Calcium-8.9 Phos-4.2 Mg-1.6
 
Brief Hospital Course:
The patient presented to ___ on ___ for medical optimization 
of her pulmonary function prior to her surgery. Pulmonary team 
was consulted and followed the patient throughout her stay. 
Anesthesia was also consulted regarding anesthesia plan. She 
underwent excision melanoma left anteri

diagnosis.details: Invasive nodular melanoma of left anterior shoulder, depth 3.15 mm, Clark level IV, with metastatic involvement of left axillary lymph nodes (bulky left axillary mass).
Approximately 1 cm ulcerative lesion on the upper abdomen.

biomarkers.details: Shave biopsy of left shoulder showed minimal depth of 3.15 mm, extending to Clark level IV. Needle biopsy of left axillary mass consistent with melanoma.

treatments.name: Pre-operative medical optimization
treatments.details: Pulmonary and anesthesia consultation for severe COPD and other comorbidities prior to surgery.

Name:  ___                  Unit No:   ___

Attending: ___.
 
Chief Complaint:
R flank pain
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
* ___ guided exchange of right PCNU with right PCN

 
History of Present Illness:
___ year old man with a h/o metastatic rectal cancer, s/p 
colostomy, ileoconduit, and r nephrostomy tube, currently on 
FOLFORI and Avastin (___), who was recently admitted at 
beginning ___ with r flank pain and was treated for 
pyelonephritis with ceftazidime initially but continued to spike 
so was switcued to meropenem. Cultures grew Klebsiella sensitive 
to fluoroquinalones. ___ changed his perc neph cath ___. He 
was switched to ciprofloxacin and discharged with close follow 
up. His suppressive regimen was changed to methanamine 1gram 
bid. Pt now presents with fever and some suprapubic discomfort 
for one day. In ED symptoms have improved. physical exam 
revealed temp of and stable bp. mild suprapubic pain without 
evidence of rebound. labs revealed positive ua and nl wcc. 
cultures were sent and iv cipro was started. pt's admitted for 
further care. 
 
Past Medical History:
PAST ONCOLOGIC HISTORY:  
======================  
- Initially presented with rectal bleeding ___ colonoscopy 
showed large tumor  
- Pre-op chemotherapy (Mt. ___) with ___ infusion pump  
- Underwent low anterior resection ___  
- Tumor T3N1  
- Received adjuvant chemo (FOLFOX)  
- ___ developed abdominal pain; found to have left 
hydronephrosis (secondary to tumor)  
- Surgery ___, (colostomy + intraoperative radiation)  
- Treated w/ Erbitux (per pt, although per OMR note by Dr. ___, 
___ got ___ & CPT-11 (completed ___  
- PET CT in ___ showed no evidence of disease  
- PET CT in ___ showed pelvic recurrence  
- Surgery ___ showed sacral disease; surgery ___ with 
pelvic exenteration, bladder removal, ileoconduit, revised 
colostomy, removed part of sacrum; course c/b large pelvic 
abscess, now s/p drainage  
- PET ___ showed uptake in right acetabulum and left ischium, 
with residual uptake in pelvic area; biopsy c/w adenocarcinoma 
metastatic from rectal CA  
- ___ had Cyberknife to two bony metastases (right 
acetabulum, left ischium)  
- ___ had right-sided percutaneous nephrostomy tube placed 
for right sided hydroureter (with + pyelo, growing Citrobacter)  

- ___ FLOX  
- ___ C1D1 FOLFOX + Avastin  
- ___ noted to have left hydronephrosis, concerning for 
pyelo; no percutaneous nephrostomy tube placed  
- s/p 9 cycles FOLFOX + Avastin  
- most recently started FOLFIRI and Avastin ___  
.  
.  
PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:  
====================  
1. Breast lumps status post multiple biopsies on the right,  
which were shown to be benign gynecomastia.  
2. Status post hernia repair in ___.  
3. Status post finger surgery.  
4. TIA in ___ and ___.  
5. Mitral valve prolapse.  
6. Atrial septal defect.  
7. Depression.  
8. UTIs -- on tetracycline prophylactically

___
Family History:
Great uncle with colon cancer at ___. Mother died of ___ 
father died at ___ and had a meningioma. Brother and sister

Physical Exam:

HEENT: EOMI, PERRLA, anicteric sclera, pink conjunctiva, MMM,

CARDIAC: RRR, S1/S2, no mrg  
LUNG: CTAB  
ABDOMEN: nondistended, active bowel sounds, soft, nontender, 
colostomy on left and ileal conduit on right both C/D/I  
GU: right nephrostomy tube in place, draining clear urine, 
moderate R CVA tenderness  
PULSES: 2+ DP pulses bilaterally  

 
Pertinent Results:
___ 12:00AM BLOOD WBC-3.6* RBC-3.00* Hgb-9.1* Hct-26.2* 
MCV-88 MCH-30.2 MCHC-34.5 RDW-17.4* Plt ___
___ 12:00AM BLOOD WBC-2.7* RBC-2.65*

Name:  ___                  Unit No:   ___
 
Admission Date:  ___              Discharge Date:   ___
 
Date of Birth:  ___             Sex:   M
 
Service: MEDICINE
 
Allergies: 
No Known Allergies / Adverse Drug Reactions
 
Attending: ___.
 
Chief Complaint:
Dyspnea
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
None

 
History of Present Illness:
___ yo man with a history of  
recurrent/advanced ___ s/p chemo/XRT, cardiomyopathy (unclear  

etiology, maybe from chemo, EF 40%), DVT s/p IVC filter, CVA, 
COPD, HTN, DM who woke up at 2 AM with chest discomfort that 
started in abdomen and moved up to his chest. This is associated 
with shortness of breath and dizziness. The episode lasted about 
a couple hours and was not relieved with TUMS. Patient reports 
cough and progressively worsening dyspnea but denies fevers, 
chills, chest pain, abdominal pain, dysuria, syncope. Patient 
has history of DVTs and has an IVC filter in place.  

He takes baby aspirin each day but is not on any other 
anticoagulation.  

He otherwise reports good exercise tolerance. Takes daily walks 
around a park. Denies exertional chest discomfort or shortness 
of breath. No orthopnea, sleeps on one pillow. No lower 
extremity edema. No recent changes in weight.  

In regards to ___, his last cycle of palliative chemo was on 
___. According to most recent heme/onc note, he has signs of 
disease progression and further options are limited and appears 
that an approach of supportive measures is being initiated. He 
has a chest CT scheduled for tomorrow.  

In the ED initial vitals were: 98.2 108 111/66 16 95% RA  
EKG: read as HR 103, low voltage,  
CXR- unchanged from prior, BNP 4515  
trop- 0.3  
guaiac- neg  
CTA was performed which was negative for PE. Head CT was 
negative for hemorrhagic mets and he was started on a heparin 
drip.  
On arrival to the floor, patient is feeling well with no 
complaints. He wants to know when he can go home.  
 
 
Past Medical History:
PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:  
1. CARDIAC RISK FACTORS: Dyslipidemia, Hypertension  
2. CARDIAC HISTORY: none  
3. OTHER PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:  
- ___, on cycle 14 of pemetrexed. diagnosed ___ with stage  
IIIB disease. started on etoposide, cisplatin, XRT on ___  

-DVT status post filter placement in ___.  
-Hypertension  
-Macular degeneration  
-H/O CVA ___, TIA ___  
-COPD  
-AAA s/p endovascular repair  
 
Social History:
___
Family History:
No family history of early MI, does have a brother with a "bad 
heart" that also beats slow  
 
 
Physical Exam:
Initial Physical Exam
================
VS: T=98.3 BP=102/64 HR=95 RR=22 O2 sat= 97RA  
GENERAL: in NAD. Oriented x3. Mood, affect appropriate.  
HEENT: NCAT. Sclera anicteric. PERRL, EOMI. Conjunctiva were 
pink, no pallor or cyanosis of the oral mucosa. No xanthelasma.  

NECK: Supple with JVP of 6 cm.  
CARDIAC: PMI located in ___ intercostal space, midclavicular 
line. RR, normal S1, S2. No m/r/g. No thrills, lifts. No S3 or 
S4.  
LUNGS: inspiratory crackles and wheezes heard in all lung 
fields, no using as accessory muscles  
ABDOMEN: Soft, NTND. No HSM or tenderness. Abd aorta not 
enlarged by palpation. No abdominal bruits.  
EXTREMITIES: No c/c/e. No femoral bruits.  
SKIN: No ulcers, scars, or xanthomas.  
PULSES: 1+ DP pulses b/l  

Discharge Physical Exam
================
GENERAL: in NAD. Oriented x3. Mood, affect appropriate.  
HEENT: NCAT. Sclera anicteric. PERRL, EOMI. Conjunctiva were 
pink, no pallor or cyanosis of the oral mucosa. No xanthelasma.  

NECK: Supple with JVP of 6 cm.  
CARDIAC: PMI located in ___ intercostal space, midclavicular 
line. RR, normal S1, S2. No m/r/g. No thrills, lifts. No S3 or 
S4.  
LUNGS: inspiratory crackles and wheezes heard in all lung 
fields, no using as accessory muscles  
ABDOMEN: Soft, NTND. No HSM or tenderness. Abd aorta not 
enlarged by palpation. No abdominal bruits.  
EXTREMITIES: No c/c/e. No femoral bruits.  
SKIN: No ulcers, scars, or xanthomas.  
PULSES: 1+ DP pulses b/l  
 
Pertine

diagnosis: Recurrent/Advanced Malignancy (likely lung cancer, stage IIIB at diagnosis) with disease progression
Acute dyspnea/heart failure exacerbation (elevated BNP, inspiratory crackles, negative for PE)
Chronic conditions: DVT status post IVC filter, CVA/TIA, COPD, Hypertension, Diabetes Mellitus, Dyslipidemia
biomarkers: CT Angiogram: Negative for pulmonary embolism
Head CT: Negative for hemorrhagic metastases
treatments: Palliative chemotherapy (pemetrexed) - recently completed cycle 14, now transitioning to supportive care
Anticoagulation: Baby aspirin (home), Heparin drip (initiated during admission)

Name:  ___.               Unit No:   ___
 
Admission Date:  ___              Discharge Date:   ___
 
Date of Birth:  ___             Sex:   F
 
Service: SURGERY
 
Allergies: 
latex / benzydamine / nasocort
 
Attending: ___.
 
Chief Complaint:
GIST tumor
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
lap gastric wedge resection - ___ ___

 
History of Present Illness:
___ is a ___ w/ hx of HTN who is presenting
here to clinic for evaluation of GIST in gastric fundus. She
recently underwent a CT A/P on ___ for evaluation of pelvic
pain and was found to have uncomplicated sigmoid diverticulitis
(which was managed outpatient w/ PO abx) and a 2 cm mass in
gastric fundus was incidentally noted. She then underwent 
EGD/EUS
___ which showed a 1.8 x 1.4 cm submucosal lesion in gastric
fundus, bx of which was c/w GIST. She had some tarry stools 
which
she attributed to her PO abx for diverticulitis and some mild
lower abd pain, but o/w has no other sx, including f/c/s, abd
distension, n/v, or BRBPR; ROS is o/w -ve except as noted 
before.

 
Past Medical History:
See OMR
 
Social History:
___
Family History:
Fam Hx: father - esophageal cancer

 
Physical Exam:
Gen: NAD, AxOx3
Card: RRR
Pulm: no respiratory distress
Abd: Soft, mild appropriate-tender, non-distended, normal bs.  
Wounds: c/d/I. dressings removed
Ext: No edema, warm well-perfused
 
Brief Hospital Course:
The patient presented on ___ for a laparoscopic gastric wedge 
resection. There were no adverse events in the operating room; 
please see the operative note for details. Post-operatively the 
patient was taken to the PACU until stable and then transferred 
to the wards until stable to go home.  
 #SURGICAL COURSE:
 #NEURO: The patient was alert and oriented throughout 
hospitalization; pain was initially managed with IV medications 
prior to transition to PO medications on POD1. Pain was very 
well controlled.  
 #CV: The patient remained stable from a cardiovascular 
standpoint; vital signs were routinely monitored.  
 #PULMONARY: The patient remained stable from a pulmonary 
standpoint; vital signs were routinely monitored. Good pulmonary 
toilet, early ambulation and incentive spirometry were 
encouraged throughout hospitalization.  
 #GI/GU/FEN: The patient had a foley placed intra-operatively, 
which was removed post-surgery on POD with autonomous return of 
voiding. The patient was tolerating a regular diet prior to 
discharge.  
 #ID: The patient's fever curves were closely watched for signs 
of infection, of which there were none.  
 #HEME: Patient received BID SQH for DVT prophylaxis, in 
addition to encouraging early ambulation and Venodyne 
compression devices.  
 --------------------  
 At the time of discharge, the patient was doing well, afebrile 
with stable vital signs. The patient was tolerating diet as 
above per oral, ambulating, voiding without assistance, and pain 
was well controlled. The patient was discharged home without 
services. The patient received discharge teaching and follow-up 
instructions with understanding verbalized and agreement with 
the discharge plan.  

 
Medications on Admission:
See OMR
 
Discharge Medications:
1.  Acetaminophen 1000 mg PO Q8H  
2.  Docusate Sodium 100 mg PO BID  
3.  OxyCODONE (Immediate Release) ___ mg PO Q4H:PRN Pain - 
Moderate 
RX *oxycodone 5 mg 1 tablet(s) by mouth q4hr prn Disp #*10 
Tablet Refills:*0 
4.  Senna 8.6 mg PO BID:PRN Constipation - First Line  
5.  Aspirin 81 mg PO DAILY  
6.  Atorvastatin 40 mg PO QPM  
7.  Chlorthalidone 25 mg PO DAILY  
8.  Losartan Potassium 100 mg PO DAILY  
9.  Metoprolol Succinate XL 100 mg PO DAILY  

 
Discharge Disposition:
Home
 
Discharge Diagnosis:
GIST

 
Discharge Condition:
Mental Status: Clear and coherent.
Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive.
Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.

 
Discharge Instructions:
 Dear Ms ___,  
 It was a pleasure taking care of you here at ___ 
___. You were admitted to our hospital for 
a laparascopic gastric wedge rese

golden_labels.diagnosis: Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumor (GIST) of the gastric fundus
biomarkers: Diagnostic biopsy consistent with GIST (specific molecular markers not reported in note)

Name:  ___                  Unit No:   ___
 
Admission Date:  ___              Discharge Date:   ___
 
Date of Birth:  ___             Sex:   M
 
Service: MEDICINE
 
Allergies: 
Sulfa (Sulfonamide Antibiotics) / Penicillins / ragweed pollen
 
Attending: ___.
 
Chief Complaint:
HiDAC treatment, Mantle Cell Lymphoma
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
none

 
History of Present Illness:
HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:  
___ is a ___ year old man with a history of Mantle 
Cell
Lymphoma (s/p 3 cycles rituximab/bendamustine with PET:3
indicating Complete Response with ___ 1, enrolled in
clinical trial protocol ___ s/p C2 of R-HiDAC.), bipolar
disorder, tobacco use disorder, who presents for planned C2 of
R-HiDAC administration. 

He was recently admitted ___ for R-HiDAC
administration and did well. He was again admitted 
___
with febrile neutropenia, treated with broad spectrum
antibiotics, but without any source identified. 

Today, he reports that he feels well. No complaints or concerns. 


 
Past Medical History:
~ ___: Feels a lump in his left neck. At the time there was 
no
fever or weight loss. He did have "mild night sweats" once every
___ weeks for the preceding year. He was eventually referred to
ENT, who performed first a Fine Needle Aspiration that was
suggestive of Lymphoma.
- ___: Excisional biopsy of the left supraclavicular node at
___ demonstrates Mantle Cell Lymphoma, with B cells
co-expressing CD20, BCL2, CD5, Cyclin D1, and Sox11. The Ki67
proliferation index was ___, with less than 1% of cells
positive for p53. 
- ___: Referred to Dr. ___ ___ ___. Plan to
obtain a PET to confirm disease extent. 
- ___: PET at ___ reveals FDG avid lymphadenopathy
involving the left greater than  right cervical chain, left
supraclavicular lymph nodes, porta hepatis,  peripancreatic and
additional retroperitoneal lymph nodes. 
- ___: Given low proliferative index and absence of poor risk
cytogenetics, Dr. ___ for close interval imaging
follow-up. 
- ___: Surveillance CT neck/torso at ___ shows ongoing
left lower neck and supraclavicular nodal disease, not increased
from PET CT, as well as right upper/retroperitoneal adenopathy
similar in scope and size to PET CT. No new adenopathy or 
lesions
were identified.
- ___: Referred to Dr. ___ at ___ for second
opinion, with plan to repeat PET scan an possibly re-biopsy.  
- ___: PET shows persistent FDG avid lymphadenopathy in the
neck, thoracic inlet, and retroperitoneum which has grown and
appears more avid in the abdomen, as well as new splenomegaly
with diffuse FDG avidity suggestive of splenic involvement, and 
a
new FDG-avid opacity in the right lung base (favored to 
represent
atelectasis or a small focus of infectious/inflammatory 
process).

- ___: Repeat biopsy of retroperitoneal adenopathy reveals
persistent Mantle Cell Lymphoma but with increased Ki-67
proliferation fraction of ___. This yields a MIPI score of
6.5, indicating Intermediate Risk. 
- ___: Bone marrow biopsy obtained for baseline assessment.
This preliminarily indicates minimal involvement by Mantle Cell
Lymphoma. Cytogenetics reveal two related neoplastic clones, one
with a derivative chromosome 8 and one with t(3;5), i7p,
t(11;14), and a pseudo-dicentric (15;6). By FISH 30% of cells
have t(11;14). 
- ___: C1D1 rituximab/bendamustine. 
- ___: C2D1 rituximab/bendamustine. Pegfilgrastim added
because of mild leukopenia. 
- ___: C3D1 rituximab/bendamustine with pegfilgrastim.
- ___: PET:3 reveals interval decrease in FDG-avidity of the
left cervical lymph nodes, left supraclavicular nodes, and
aortocaval/portocaval lymph nodes, and decrease in size and
FDG-avidity of the spleen. Overall, this is a ___ score of
1, consistent with a Complete Response.  

  
PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:  
- Mantle Cell Lymphoma, as above
- Tobacco Dependence 
- Bipolar Disorder
- Hyperlipidemia 

 
Social History:
___
Family History:
Brother has a history of arrhythmia. Father is
alive had colon cancer and diabetes. An uncle had some type

biomarkers.ki67: Noted (specific value redacted), increased on repeat biopsy

Name:  ___.              Unit No:   ___
 
Admission Date:  ___              Discharge Date:   ___
 
Date of Birth:  ___             Sex:   M
 
Service: MEDICINE
 
Allergies: 
Sulfonamides / Wellbutrin / Tape
 
Attending: ___.
 
Chief Complaint:
Rigors
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
None

 
History of Present Illness:
The patient is a ___ year old male with chief complaint of 
rigors. Has recurrent papillary renal cell carcinoma. Had stent 
placed several months ago and has had hematuria since (on 
coumadin for afib). Felt fatigued yesterday and weak. Had rigors 
last night. Never checked a temp. Today was at regularly 
rescheduled oncology appt, still fatigued but developed rigors 
in waiting room. Received 1 dose of ceftriaxone in the clinic. 
VS in clinic T 98.2, BP 152/121, HR 93. Sent to ED for further 
evaluation.

In the ED, initial vitals were 100.8 136/80 86 24 98%RA. UA 
grossly positive. Spiked to 100.9 while in the ED. While in the 
ED received couamdin 4mg, synthroid 75mcg, metoprolol 25mg, and 
ibuprofen 400mg all x 1. Also received 4L IVF. Had already 
received ceftriaxone in the ___ clinic. Admitted to OMED.
 
Past Medical History:
1) Metastatic papillary renal cell cancer (as below)
2) Hydronephrosis 
3) Pacemaker placement (___)
4) Hypothyroidism
5) Hypertension
6) AF ablation
7) Colonic polyps

ONCOLOGIC HISTORY:
- ___: Patient had sudden onset of mid-back pain. Xray, and 
subsequent CT showed a large lytic T8 lesion abutting the spinal 
cord. A small right kidney lesion was also noted. Biopsy of the 
T8 lesion on ___ revealed poorly differentiated carcinoma 
with marked degenerative changes. Tumor cells stained for 
keratin cocktail, consistent with an epithelial origin, but did 
not stain for CK7. Tumor cells stain for CD10 and p504s. This 
staining pattern is consistent with a carcinoma of renal origin 
but is not specific and does not exclude another primary source.
- ___: Patient underwent T8 vertebrectomy. Pathology was 
positive for cytokeratin cocktail and focally positive for CD10, 
negative for CK7, CK20, TTF-1, PSA, and PSAP. This pattern was 
consistent with a renal primary
- ___: XRT to the spine
- ___: Right partial nephrectomy and LN dissection. 
Pathology revealed a grade 3 papillary renal cell carcinoma, 2.1 
cm in greatest dimension, extending into perinephric tissues. 
___ LN were positive for metastatic disease. Margins were 
negative, there were no mets to the adrenal, and LVI was absent.
 
Social History:
___
Family History:
Father had stroke in ___. Mother had colitis. Sister has cardiac 
valvular disease. 
 
Physical Exam:
Vitals: 99.4 118/76 94 20 95%RA
General: patient diaphoretic, but no acute distress
HEENT: PERRL, EOMI
CV: RRR
Resp: CTA B/L
Abd: nontender, nondistended
Ext: no edema
 
Pertinent Results:
RENAL U.S.Study Date of ___:
FINDINGS: The right kidney measures 11.1 cm in length. The left 
kidney measures 12.6 cm. Except for mild pelviectasis in the 
upper pole of the right kidney, which has not progressed since 
the prior study, each kidney is unremarkable without stones, 
masses, or hydronephrosis. The in situ stent can be partly 
visualized in the central right renal collecting system, 
although the medial aspect of each pyeloureteral junction is not 
well visualized because of overlying bowel gas. Within the 
partly full bladder, the distal pigtail is visualized. Please 
note that the mid-ureters are not visualized by ultrasound.
IMPRESSION: No hydronephrosis. Stent within the right ureter.

ADMISSION AND DISCHARGE LABORATORY RESULTS:
___ 04:12PM BLOOD WBC-9.6# RBC-4.31* Hgb-13.9* Hct-40.3 
MCV-94 MCH-32.3* MCHC-34.5 RDW-13.0 Plt ___
___ 08:57AM BLOOD ___
___ 08:10AM BLOOD WBC-4.1 RBC-3.94* Hgb-12.5* Hct-36.8* 
MCV-93 MCH-31.8 MCHC-34.1 RDW-12.8 Plt ___
___ 08:10AM BLOOD ___ PTT-26.3 ___
___ 07:20AM BLOOD Glucose-135* UreaN-14 Creat-0.9 Na-139 
K-4.0 Cl-108 HCO3-25 AnGap-10
___ 04:12PM BLOOD ALT-12 AST-19 LD(LDH)-180 AlkPhos-62 
TotBili-1.3
___ 04:12PM BLOOD C

diagnosis.secondary: Acute infection (likely UTI/sepsis) in setting of ureteral stent and hematuria

past: Right partial nephrectomy with lymph node dissection

original_text:
Name:  ___                  Unit No:   ___
 
Admission Date:  ___              Discharge Date:   ___
 
Date of Birth:  ___             Sex:   M
 
Service: MEDICINE
 
Allergies: 
Sulfa (Sulfonamide Antibiotics) / Iodinated Contrast Media - IV 
Dye
 
Attending: ___
 
Chief Complaint:
chemotherapy initiation
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
Bone marrow biopsy ___
PICC placement ___
Lumbar puncture ___

 
History of Present Illness:
Mr ___ is a ___ man who is admitted from Dr. ___ 
for urgent evaluation and treatment of new diagnosis of 
lymphoma.

His symptoms first started in ___, when he developed right 
shoulder and periscapular pain after doing some yard work. The 
pain subsided with over the counter medication. On ___, he 
had a MRI shoulder and was re-evaluated by Dr. ___. MRI was 
concerning for a lytic lesion in the shoulder. He had a CT 
chest, abdomen, and pelvis on ___. CT chest showed no 
nodules, but showed an enlarged main pulmonary artery suggesting 
pulmonary hypertension. The left iliac bone showed a 1cm mixed 
lytic & sclerotic lesion in the left internal iliac lymph node 
chain. Also showed several lymph nodes in left internal iliach 
chain, the largest was 1 cm. On ___, he had bone marrow 
biopsy that showed atypical B-cell dominiant lymphoid with 
extensive necrosis, consistent with large B-cell lymphoma. He 
also had a bone scan on ___, showing increased uptake in 
right humeral head and proximal diaphysis, as well as irregular 
uptake throughout bilateral ribs. There was a concern for this 
being a metastatic prostate cancer due to chronically elevated 
PSA, but patient had normal MRI of prostate. Of note, ___ was 
835 on ___ and creatinine was 1.3.

He saw Dr. ___ in clinic today (___), and is being 
admitted for urgent evaluation and treatment of his lymphoma.

In clinic today, initial vitals: T 98.5 HR 60 BP 103/66 RR 16 Wt 
166 pounds

Exam notable for: rotator cuff pathology and bilateral shoulder 
pain, but a normal neurovascular exam of both upper extremities 
as in previous visits.

- Labs notable for: 
* WBC 5.9 (78.7% neutrophils, 10.4% lymph)
* Na 140, K 4.4, Cl 101, HCO3 29, BUN 22, Cr 1.2, Ca 9.8, Mg 
2.4, P 4.1 
* ALT 38 AST 30 AP 240 (high) LDH 1143 (high) t bili 0.5
* IgG 851, IgA 124, IgM 62
* Hepatitis serologies & HIV pending

- Imaging notable for: PET scan showing diffuse bone 
involvement. (Exact read written below)
 
On arrival to the floor, patient is feeling very anxious about 
his new diagnosis. He is accompanied by his wife. He denies any 
recent fevers, chills, night sweats, lymphadenopathy, pruritis, 
or weight loss. He endorses some vision changes for the past 
year, and pain in his right shoulder for the past few months. 
Otherwise, he has felt well recently. 

Prior to this afternoon's bone marrow biopsy, patient developed 
chills and rigors. He received Tylenol, Morphine, and Demerol, 
and these symptoms resolved. He tolerated the procedure well, 
and is currently in no pain. 

Review of Systems:  
(+) Per HPI. Also + for vision changes, chronic urinary 
retention, and recent nausea after drinking contrast for CT 
scans, which has since resolved. Also chronic low back pain, no 
recent changes.
Denies fever, chills, night sweats, headache, rhinorrhea, 
congestion, sore throat, cough, shortness of breath, chest pain, 
abdominal pain, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation, BRBPR, melena, 
hematochezia, dysuria, hematuria. 

 
Past Medical History:
- Lymphoma, as above 
- Hip pain, s/p hip replacement in ___
- Achilles tendinitis on R
- Gout 

 
Social History:
___
Family History:
Mother died of melanoma. Paternal aunt died of ___ 
lymphoma.

 
Physical Exam:
ADMISSION PHYSICAL EXAM:
Vitals: T 98.7 HR 68 BP 120/72 RR 18 SaO2 100% on ra
Gen: Pleasant, a little anxious, but non-toxic   
HEENT: No conjunctival pallor. No icterus. MMM. OP clear.  
NECK: No JVD Normal carotid upstroke without bruits. 
LYMPH: No cervical, axillary, or supraclav LAD
CV: Normocardic, regular. Normal

diagnosis.name: Large B-cell lymphoma
diagnosis.details: Diagnosed by bone marrow biopsy showing atypical B-cell dominant lymphoid with extensive necrosis. Imaging shows diffuse bone involvement.

biomarkers.name: Prostate-Specific Antigen (PSA)
biomarkers.note: Chronically elevated, prompted concern for metastatic prostate cancer, but prostate MRI was normal.

treatments.name: Chemotherapy
treatments.details: Admission for urgent initiation of chemotherapy for large B-cell lymphoma.

treatments.name: Analgesia
treatments.details: Tylenol, Morphine, Demerol given for pain/chills related to biopsy procedure.

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
___: Right Crani for Frontal Hemorrhagic mass
___: OR placement of EVD drain

 
History of Present Illness:
___ with h/o melanoma and recent LEEP awoke this morning with 
severe headache and slurred speech. EMS to OSH with progressive 
neurologic decline requiring intubation.  Ct showed large right 
frontoparietal hemorrhage with extension into right lateral 
ventricle with casting into ___ and ___. Also several lesions
present.

 
Past Medical History:
melanoma with excision of lesion on back ___ Grave's disease
 
Social History:
___
Family History:
non-contributory
 
Physical Exam:
Upon Admission:
PHYSICAL EXAM:
O: T: 98.8 BP: 123/60   HR:97    R 16  O2Sats100%
Gen: WD/WN, comfortable, NAD.
HEENT: proptosis  Pupils:2.5mm R 3 mm L   EOMs nystagmus
Neck: Supple.
Extrem: Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro:intubated examined in ED, opens eyes to voice and follws
commands on right.  attempts to lift ___ to noxious, minimal
movement to noxious L UE
Toes upgoing right, mute left

Upon Discharge:
AOx3. Afebrile for 24+ hours. MAE ___. Difficulty with upper 
gaze but improved. head incision is C/D/I
 
Pertinent Results:
CT Head ___:
IMPRESSION: 
1. Large right frontoparietal hemorrhage, with vasogenic edema, 
extending 
into the ventricles. Subfalcine herniation. 
2. Multiple foci of hyperdensity with round shape in the brain, 
concerning 
for possible underlying secondary deposits. Please correlate 
with history of primary cancer if available. MRI is recommended 
for further evaluation of the underlying disease. 

CT Head ___:
Status post right craniectomy for intraparenchymal right frontal 
hemorrhage. No new foci of intraparenchymal hemorrhage. Expected 
postoperative changes with pneumocephalus and high-attenuation 
material, likely blood at the operative site. Decreased leftward 
shift to 2 mm. 

MRI Brain ___:
Post-op changes seen.

CT Torso ___:
CT OF THE CHEST WITH CONTRAST: Bi-apical paraseptal emphysema 
and scarring is present. Multiple nodules are noted within the 
lung parenchyma, including 1-2 mm lesion within the right upper 
lobe (3:15), suspicious probable metastatic lesion within the 
right lower lobe measuring 6.5 x 7 mm (3:29) and 2.5-mm lesion 
within the left lower lobe (3:39). Dependent linear and nodular 
atelectasis is noted. There is no pleural or pericardial 
effusion. Heart and great vessels appear unremarkable. No 
pathologically enlarged central or axillary lymph nodes are 
present. Endotracheal tube and nasogastric tube are in situ and 
appropriately located. 
  
CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITHOUT AND WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST AND WITH 
ORAL 
CONTRAST: The liver, gallbladder, spleen, stomach, small bowel, 
pancreas, 
kidneys, and right adrenal gland are unremarkable. A 12 x 13 mm 
left adrenal lesion is noted which displays Hounsfield 
attenuation values of ___ on the un-enhanced images, suggestive 
of an adenoma. No pathologically enlarged retroperitoneal or 
mesenteric lymph nodes are present. Atherosclerotic disease is 
present within the intra-abdominal aorta. 
  
CT OF THE PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS AND ORAL CONTRAST: There are 
findings of 
left-sided pelvic congestion with reflux of contrast and 
dilatation of the 
left gonadal vein extending into the periuterine plexus. The 
uterus and 
adnexa appear otherwise unremarkable. Air is present within a 
Foley 
containing urinary bladder. Intrapelvic bowel appears normal, no 

pathologically enlarged sidewall or inguinal lymph nodes are 
present. A 
suspicious enhancing soft tissue focus is noted within the left 
gluteal 
subcutaneous fat immediately abutting the adjacent gluteal 
musculature 
measuring 8 x 15 mm (3:80). No additional intramuscular or s

diagnosis.site: Brain (multiple hemorrhagic metastases), Lungs, Left gluteal subcutaneous tissue
diagnosis.details: History of primary melanoma. Presenting with large right frontoparietal hemorrhagic metastasis causing intraparenchymal hemorrhage, intraventricular extension, vasogenic edema, and subfalcine herniation. Additional suspicious lesions in lungs and left gluteal region.

biomarkers.details: No molecular biomarkers (e.g., BRAF, NRAS, PD-L1) are reported in this clinical note.

treatments.details: Right craniectomy for evacuation of right frontal intraparenchymal hemorrhage and placement of external ventricular drain (EVD) for hydrocephalus/ventricular casting.

original_text:
Name:  ___                    Unit No:   ___
 
Admission Date:  ___              Discharge Date:   ___
 
Date of Birth:  ___             Sex:   F
 
Service: MEDICINE
 
Allergies: 
alcohol
 
Attending: ___.
 
Chief Complaint:
Right knee weakness
 
Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
None

 
History of Present Illness:
Ms. ___ is an ___ woman with a past medical history of 
hypertension and left knee replacement in ___ and a three-week 
history of cough who presents with acute weakness of her right 
knee.

She first noticed the weakness of her right leg, specifically 
the knee, at home on the morning of ___ when she could not 
stand or put weight on it. She struggled to walk to the 
bathroom, and it took her 2 hours to be able to rise from the 
toilet. This weakness continued into the afternoon. Finally at 2 
pm a repairman came to her house and helped her get to the 
hospital. 

She has had no recent falls, no trauma to the leg. She has no 
pain - just discomfort and resistance to movement. No 
lightheadedness, no weakness or pain anywhere else. She does 
have a productive cough that she has had for 3 weeks associated 
with hoarseness, shortness-of-breath, rib cage discomfort from 
coughing, and a low-grade fever that she has been treated for by 
her PCP. No runny nose, nasal congestion, headache, chills, 
rashes, urinary issues, changes in bowel movements, abdominal 
pain. 

Upon arrival to the ED she was febrile to 101 with a HR of 122 
and BP of 176/77, breathing 96% on room air. Her right knee was 
warm, swollen, and was severely painful with movement. Labs were 
notable for a WBC of 22.9, BUN/Cr of 33/1.8, Lactate of 2.4, and 
CRP > 300. A chest radiograph revealed a right basilar opacity 
consistent with consolidation. Knee radiograph showed a small 
suprapatellar effusion. Ortho was consulted and performed 
arthrocentesis that showed 77K WBCs and rhomboid crystals with 
positive birefringence. Gram stain showed 4+ PMNs without 
microorganisms. She was given normal saline, ceftriazone and 
azithromycin for pneumonia, vancomycin for possible septic 
arthritis, and Tylenol for pain.

This morning she is comfortable in bed with a productive cough 
and shortness of breath. Her knee is weak and tender to 
palpation and movement but not painful at rest. No fevers or 
chills.
 
Past Medical History:
Hypertension
Stage IC Sertoli cell tumor of the ovary s/p resection
L Knee Replacement ___
 
Social History:
___
Family History:
She denies any family history of cancer.

 
Physical Exam:
====================
EXAM ON ADMISSION
====================
Vitals: 98.3 120/45 95 18 96%RA
General: Alert, oriented, no acute distress
HEENT: Sclera anicteric, MMM, oropharynx clear, EOMI, PERRL
Neck: Supple, JVP not elevated, no LAD
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, normal S1 + S2, no murmurs, rubs, 
gallops
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally, no wheezes, rales, 
rhonchi
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended, bowel sounds present, 
no organomegaly, no rebound or guarding
Ext/Neuro: R knee with some swelling and warmth, limited ROM d/t 
pain, L knee and other ___ joints without issue & w/full ROM, 
intact strength, UE joints w/o abnormalities, face symmetric.

====================
EXAM ON DISCHARGE
====================
Vitals: T 97.8 BP 146/70 HR 94 RR 18 94% on room air 
General: Alert, oriented, no acute distress  
HEENT: Sclera anicteric, MMM, oropharynx clear  
Neck: JVP not elevated, no LAD  
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. Low lung volumes. No 
wheezes or rhonchi. 
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, normal S1 + S2. No murmurs, rubs, 
gallops  
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended, bowel sounds present, 
no rebound tenderness or guarding. Periumbilical scar. 
Ext: Warm, well perfused, 2+ pulses, no clubbing, cyanosis or 
edema. Left knee with midline scar. Right knee still moderately 
swollen. No warmth or erythema. Able to flex knee actively. 
Right and left hip flexion, plantarflexion, dorsiflexion ___. 
Right hamstring ___.

diagnosis: Acute calcium pyrophosphate crystal arthritis (pseudogout) of the right knee
History of Sertoli cell tumor of the ovary status post resection

imaging: Chest radiograph: right basilar opacity consistent with consolidation
